title: Agent Memory: Short vs. Long Term

mental_model.intuition: Short-term memory is RAM (the context window); long-term memory is a hard drive (a database).
mental_model.description: An LLM is inherently stateless. 'Memory' is just data you choose to inject into its prompt. Short-term memory is the literal array of recent chat messages sent in the current request. Long-term memory involves saving important facts to a database (often a vector DB) and actively retrieving them in future sessions.

sections.body: Because LLMs have no internal memory across API calls, to have a 'conversation', you must send the entire history of messages back to the model every single time. This running list of messages is the agent's short-term memory.
sections.recap: So far: Short-term memory is just appending messages to an array and resending them.

sections.body: Eventually, short-term memory hits the model's token limit, or becomes too expensive and slow to process. You must trim it. A common strategy is a 'sliding window'—keeping only the last 10 messages and discarding the rest. But this gives the agent amnesia for older details.
sections.recap: So far: The context window is finite, so old messages must be dropped.

sections.body: To remember things across weeks or dropped sessions, you need long-term memory. Instead of keeping everything in the prompt, you write important facts (like 'user prefers Python') to a database. When a new session starts, you query the database and inject only the relevant facts into the system prompt.
sections.recap: Long-term memory is just a database retrieval pipeline (RAG) applied to user facts.

code_snippets.title: Short-term memory (Sliding Window)
code_snippets.code:
MAX_MESSAGES = 10

def chat_with_memory(new_user_text, message_history):
    # Append new message
    message_history.append({'role': 'user', 'content': new_user_text})
    
    # Sliding window: keep only the system prompt + last N messages
    system_prompt = message_history[0]
    recent_history = message_history[-MAX_MESSAGES:]
    truncated_history = [system_prompt] + recent_history
    
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        messages=truncated_history
    )
    
    message_history.append(response.choices[0].message)
    return response
code_snippets.explanation: This keeps the token count stable. We slice the array to drop old messages, but always preserve the system prompt at index 0 so the agent doesn't forget its core instructions.

code_snippets.title: Extracting facts for Long-term memory
code_snippets.explanation: Long-term memory isn't magic; it's just a tool call. The agent decides to save a fact, your app writes it to Postgres, and on the next login, your app reads it and stuffs it into the system prompt.

common_mistakes.mistake: Thinking the model 'remembers' previous API calls.
common_mistakes.correction: Models are perfectly stateless. If you don't explicitly pass a fact in the `messages` array of the current HTTP request, the model literally cannot know it.
common_mistakes.title: Placeholder mistake title

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting the short-term memory array grow infinitely.
common_mistakes.correction: Appending messages forever will eventually crash with a context length exceeded error, and will cost a fortune in input tokens along the way. You must truncate or summarize.
common_mistakes.title: Placeholder mistake title

recall_questions.question: What is the primary limitation of short-term memory in LLM applications?
options: It is constrained by the model's maximum context window and token costs.
It gets erased every time the model generates a tool call.
It can only store system prompts, not user messages.
It is strictly limited to 5 messages by the API.
recall_questions.correct_answer: It is constrained by the model's maximum context window and token costs.
recall_questions.q: What is the primary limitation of short-term memory in LLM applications?

recall_questions.question: How do AI agents typically implement long-term memory?
options: By saving data to an external database and injecting relevant facts into future prompts.
By modifying the weights of the LLM in real-time.
By keeping a persistent WebSocket connection open indefinitely.
By relying on the LLM provider to store state across sessions.
recall_questions.correct_answer: By saving data to an external database and injecting relevant facts into future prompts.
recall_questions.q: How do AI agents typically implement long-term memory?

recall_questions.question: When implementing a sliding window for short-term memory, which message must you be careful NEVER to discard?
options: The system prompt containing the core instructions.
recall_questions.correct_answer: The system prompt containing the core instructions.
recall_questions.q: When implementing a sliding window for short-term memory, which message must you be careful NEVER to discard?

oa_questions.question: Your chatbot works great for 10 minutes, but then API requests start failing with a 400 error about 'context length'. How do you fix this?
oa_questions.answer: The `messages` array is growing infinitely and hitting the token limit. Implement a memory management strategy: either a sliding window (drop messages older than N), or summarize the conversation history (use the LLM to compress older messages into a short summary paragraph) and prepend that summary to the recent messages.
oa_questions.approach: Identify the stateless nature of LLMs and the hard limit of the context window. Propose truncation or summarization as the standard fixes.

oa_questions.question: Design a long-term memory system for a personal assistant agent that needs to remember user preferences across sessions.
oa_questions.answer: 1. Equip the agent with a `save_fact(fact_string)` tool. 2. When the user states a preference, the agent calls the tool. 3. The app embeds the fact and stores it in a Vector DB tied to the user's ID. 4. On future queries, run a semantic search on the Vector DB using the new user message, and inject the retrieved facts into the system prompt.
oa_questions.approach: Combine tool-calling (for writing memory) with RAG (for retrieving memory). Emphasize that long-term memory is just a specialized database integration.

glossary.definition: The recent chat messages you send back to the AI in every request so it remembers the current conversation.
A system where you save important facts about a user to a database and retrieve them weeks later so the AI remembers them.
A simple memory trick where you only keep the last 10 messages of a conversation and throw away the rest to save space and money.
